# Issue #8751: Use {} for better performance in dict()
**Repository:** Project-MONAI/MONAI
**Author:** benediktjohannes
**Status:** closed
**Created At:** 2026-02-22T13:48:30Z

## Description
### Description
Follow-up for #8748 and so on (I'll combine these changes in future 👍)

### Types of changes
<!--- Put an `x` in all the boxes that apply, and remove the not applicable items -->
- [x] Non-breaking change (fix or new feature that would not break existing functionality).
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or new feature that would cause existing functionality to change).
- [ ] New tests added to cover the changes.
- [ ] Integration tests passed locally by running `./runtests.sh -f -u --net --coverage`.
- [ ] Quick tests passed locally by running `./runtests.sh --quick --unittests  --disttests`.
- [ ] In-line docstrings updated.
- [ ] Documentation updated, tested `make html` command in the `docs/` folder.

### Comment by ericspod at 2026-02-27T00:12:43Z
Hi @benediktjohannes I not sure this change is really necessary. I know I merged the previous PR about replacing `tuple()` with `()` but I'm not sure that would actually do anything. Here I think `dict()` and `{}` should be the same thing, and regardless the constructor is called only the once typically. Do you actually see any speedup from this?
### Comment by benediktjohannes at 2026-04-02T16:49:57Z
> Hi @benediktjohannes I not sure this change is really necessary. I know I merged the previous PR about replacing `tuple()` with `()` but I'm not sure that would actually do anything. Here I think `dict()` and `{}` should be the same thing, and regardless the constructor is called only the once typically. Do you actually see any speedup from this?

We replaced this in #1423 e.g. in line "properties = dict()" to "properties = {}" as well, so I think it's better.
### Comment by benediktjohannes at 2026-04-02T17:28:41Z
cc @ericspod
### Comment by ericspod at 2026-04-03T22:52:46Z
I can merge this now but the thing with these minor changes is that they would only matter in high frequency code anyway. The lookup time saved only matters if the line is executed a very large number of times.
### Comment by benediktjohannes at 2026-04-03T23:20:45Z
@ericspod thanks! Could you please also merge the one in the hot loop path? This should be way more important. Thanks!
